commands.author: spacegeologist

target.title: fix(webchat): broadcast agent TTS media final payloads
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #81468.
- Bridges WebChat-managed agent-run TTS/media supplements into the final `chat` broadcast payload, so the live WebChat path receives the same audio attachment that was already appended to the transcript.
- Leaves the non-agent final assembly branch alone; this targets the agent-run path called out in the review of #81470.
- Preserves the existing transcript dedupe behavior by reusing the already-appended assistant-media message for broadcast instead of appending a second assistant transcript entry.
- Adds a running Gateway proof test that sends `chat.send` over WebSocket, observes the final `chat` event, verifies the Control UI assistant-media HTTP route can serve the MP3, and confirms `chat.history` still has one assistant media supplement.

## Verification
- `COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "chat.send broadcasts agent-run TTS audio over the running gateway" --reporter verbose`
- `COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts -t "persists auto-TTS final media as audio-only" --reporter verbose`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Agent-run auto-TTS/audio replies could be persisted as a WebChat assistant-media transcript supplement while the live final WebChat `chat` event remained text-only or omitted the audio attachment. The result was generated audio appearing in history/assistant-media state but not in the real-time final broadcast consumed by WebChat.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS at head `5f597ef6410136db31f85bb4bb0b2ec5844ebd80`, rebased onto `origin/main` `23e9bc8c0b61f37851136dc5da2311935e348f5d`. Dependencies were installed from `pnpm-lock.yaml` using project pnpm `11.2.2` via Corepack. The running-gateway proof starts the test Gateway on `127.0.0.1` with Control UI enabled and token auth.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "chat.send broadcasts agent-run TTS audio over the running gateway" --reporter verbose`
- `COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts -t "persists auto-TTS final media as audio-only" --reporter verbose`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Evidence after fix:
```txt
✓ |gateway-server| ../../src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts > gateway server chat > chat.send broadcasts agent-run TTS audio over the running gateway 784ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 29 skipped (30)
Duration  11.99s
```

The running-gateway proof exercises the user-visible delivery path and asserts these runtime facts:
```txt
Gateway request: chat.send over WebSocket
runId: idem-running-gateway-agent-tts
final event: event=chat, state=final, sessionKey=agent:main:main
message.role: assistant
message.content[0]: { type: "text", text: "Audio reply" }
message.content[1]: { type: "attachment", attachment: { kind: "audio", label: "tts.mp3", mimeType: "audio/mpeg", isVoiceNote: true } }
assistant-media metadata route: HTTP 200, available=true, mediaTicket starts with v1.
assistant-media ticketed GET: HTTP 200, byteLength=4
chat.history assistant messages after run: 1
spoken/model transcript text in final message/history: absent
```

Focused handler regression was also re-run at the same head:
```txt
✓ |gateway-client| ../../src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts > chat directive tag stripping for non-streaming final payloads > persists auto-TTS final media as audio-only so webchat does not duplicate assistant text 141ms
✓ |gateway-methods| ../../src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts > chat directive tag stripping for non-streaming final payloads > persists auto-TTS final media as audio-only so webchat does not duplicate assistant text 81ms

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  2 passed | 172 skipped (174)
Duration  5.48s
```

Observed result after fix: A running Gateway now broadcasts the agent-run auto-TTS media payload as the final WebChat assistant message with an audio attachment block. The Control UI assistant-media route can issue a ticket and serve the MP3, and the transcript remains deduplicated to one assistant media supplement entry.

What was not tested: I did not use a real Microsoft/Xiaomi TTS credential or assert browser autoplay behavior. The running proof uses a local MP3 and mocked agent dispatch, but it exercises the changed Gateway/WebSocket final `chat` event, Control UI assistant-media HTTP availability/serving path, and `chat.history` transcript dedupe in a running Gateway setup.

## AI Assistance Disclosure
This PR was prepared with AI assistance. I reviewed the changed code path and verified the targeted tests above.
